codeReview.summary: The _GlobalGraphDecodeState class correctly captures TWO HIP graph segments per GPU per layer (attention + FFN), builds the C graph dispatch plan (CGraphDispatchPlan), and provides set_global_graph_dispatch() toggle. Tests pass: all 6/6 tests in test_global_graph.py. The implementation inherits most logic from _GraphDecodeState with proper kernel P2P allreduce support added. Minor non-blocking issues noted.

issues.description: Comment says 'Between segments: kernel P2P allreduce via host (not in graph)' — this is accurate but misleading. The naming '_GlobalGraphDecodeState' implies a fully-captured global graph, but the allreduce is still host-orchestrated. This may confuse future developers expecting truly in-graph allreduce. The comment is technically accurate but the class name is aspirationally named.

The include_allreduce parameter is accepted but documented as 'ignored in this impl'. While this provides API compatibility, it could confuse callers into thinking allreduce can be disabled. A clearer approach would be to remove the parameter or raise a deprecation warning if it's False.

capture_full_layer() does not validate that build_decode_launch_cache() has been called before capturing. If called prematurely, tp._engine_layer_caches will be empty and the loop will silently produce empty graphs. Adding an assertion or check would be safer.

hipStreamCreate errors are only raised on explicit non-zero return codes, but the stream handles are stored as .value which may be 0 (NULL) on some platforms even with success. The stream handles should be validated before use in capture.

The mutable node tracking compares len(kernel_nodes) == len(capture_order) for position-based matching. If kernels are present/absent based on layer configuration (e.g., no sigmoid_mul in DeltaNet layers), this comparison might silently fall back to partial tracking. The fallback warning is printed but the logic continues — this is acceptable given tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml is missing a build_c_graph_dispatch command. The c_graph_dispatch.c build command (gcc -O3 -shared -fPIC -I/opt/rocm/include -L/opt/rocm/lib -lamdhip64 -o src/runtime/c_graph_dispatch.so src/runtime/c_graph_dispatch.c) is documented in architecture.md and used by workers, but not in services.yaml. Workers must manually construct this command each time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff and architecture.md both document this build command. Existing services.yaml has build_c_dispatch and build_c_dispatch_v2 entries but no build_c_graph_dispatch.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: Add a build_c_graph_dispatch command to services.yaml similar to the existing build_c_dispatch_v2 entry.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md documents that c_graph_dispatch.so is deleted by rsync --delete and must be rebuilt after each deploy. This is critical operational knowledge that could block future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: global-graph-c-dispatch-bench handoff: 'c_graph_dispatch.so gets deleted during rsync --delete because it is a build artifact in src/runtime/'. Worker documented this in architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: This is already documented in architecture.md which is correct — no additional action needed unless a services.yaml build command is added.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The _GlobalGraphDecodeState class is a near-duplicate of _GraphDecodeState with the same segment structure (attn + FFN segments, same mutable param tracking). The key difference is kernel P2P allreduce support and C graph dispatch plan. This duplication could be refactored into a single class with a strategy parameter, but this is a suggestion, not a problem.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Both classes in tp_engine.py have nearly identical capture_all/capture_full_layer loops. _GlobalGraphDecodeState has additional C dispatch plan building logic.
sharedStateObservations.suggestedChange: No immediate action needed — duplication is acceptable given the milestone scope. Could be flagged for future refactoring.

summary: global-graph-capture-impl passes code review. The _GlobalGraphDecodeState implementation is correct: it captures 512 segments (4 GPUs × 64 layers × 2 segments) successfully, handles both full_attention and linear_attention (DeltaNet) layer types, correctly tracks mutable parameters (cos/sin offsets, seq_len, KV cache positions), and integrates with the C graph dispatch plan. All 6/6 tests pass with min cosine_sim=0.997375 (>=0.99). The main non-blocking issue is that the allreduce is still host-orchestrated (not captured in graph), meaning throughput equals C dispatch rather than exceeding it. One services.yaml gap found: missing build_c_graph_dispatch command entry.
